title: [lichen_l2.c:845] SECURITY: TX with dst_eui64=NULL always broadcasts - no L2 unicast
description:
Security finding: lichen_link_tx() is called with dst_eui64=NULL (line 843-845), meaning all TX frames are broadcast with no L2 destination address.

Design decision documented at lines 836-843: 'LICHEN always transmits broadcast frames at L2'

Security implications:
1. All nodes in RF range receive every frame
2. No L2-level access control or filtering
3. Increases attack surface - malicious nodes see all traffic
4. Energy impact - all nodes must process all frames

The design comment claims this is intentional for mesh routing, but:
- It enables passive eavesdropping of all frame metadata
- Replay attacks are easier (attacker sees all traffic)
- DoS by flooding is more effective (all nodes process all frames)

Impact: P2 - architectural design decision with security tradeoffs. Not a bug per se, but should be documented as a security consideration.

Mitigation:
1. Document security implications in design docs
2. Consider optional unicast mode for point-to-point links
3. Ensure OSCORE (E2E encryption) is mandatory for sensitive payloads
close_reason: Documented: added SECURITY comment explaining L2 broadcast implications (eavesdropping, DoS), mitigations (OSCORE, Schnorr sigs, IPv6 filtering)
